steps.title: Spot the temporal shift
steps.content: The first sentence introduces two proposed offshore wind farms with massive projected capacities (1,866 MW and 8,200 MW), using “would generate” to describe their future potential. The second sentence shifts to what is currently true: “the most electricity generated by a single offshore wind farm is 1,218 MW.” The decisive signal is this move from speculative future proposals to established present reality.

steps.title: Match the transition word
steps.content: ”Presently” marks the shift to the current state: what has actually been achieved (1,218 MW) versus the proposed future capacities. The other choices either lack this temporal precision (C is generic contrast, D implies similarity contradicting the facts, A wrongly suggests the record is predictable). That matches choice B — B is correct.
